title: --platform does not work when using Docker CLI
body:
### Issue Description

When Podman is configured to provide `docker.sock` and the Docker CLI is used to invoke `docker run --platform=`, the platform is ignored, and it runs whatever the latest pulled image is.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ podman run --rm -it --platform=linux/amd64 ubuntu:focal arch
...
x86_64

$ podman run --rm -it --platform=linux/aarch64 ubuntu:focal arch
...
aarch64

$ docker run --rm -it --platform=linux/amd64 ubuntu:focal arch
aarch64

$ ls -l /var/run/docker.sock
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  daemon  61 Jun 20 17:04 /var/run/docker.sock -> ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman.sock
```

### Describe the results you received

The third command is incorrect; even though `linux/amd64` is specified, the architecture of the container is reported as `aarch64`.

### Describe the results you expected

Podman should see that the platform of the tagged image is not `linux/amd64` and so it should pull the tag with the correct platform.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Installed via Homebrew. macOS Ventura 13.4. Docker Desktop 24.0.2.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
The platform is sent to the API like this: `/v1.41/containers/create?platform=linux%2Famd64 HTTP/1.1`

I believe [`CreateContainer`](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a77f896babb8032f38a59d6e55862a74c85a4d6d/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers_create.go#L32) needs to do something with `query.Platform`. `cliOpts` needs to use it to populate the `ContainerCreateOptions` object.
I can confirm that the platform parameter is currently ignored by the compat API.  I will spin up a PR to fix it.  The Podman v4.6 release is just around the corner so the fix should reach you soon.
